reason: Extended upstream response-header timeout so slower backends have more time before the router fails the request.
notes: ResponseHeaderTimeout increased to 60s; connect/TLS remain 10s and expect-continue remains 5s.

reason: Changed proxy error handling to classify upstream failures into 502 vs 504 and route them through the new unavailable-page response path.
notes: Added classifyUpstreamError helper and improved logging with status codes.

reason: Made the upstream-failure HTML page dynamic so it can render the actual returned status code and status text instead of always showing a 503 page.
notes: Adds Retry-After for gateway timeouts.

reason: Added focused tests to verify timeout errors map to 504 and connection failures map to 502.
notes: Covers the new error classification behavior.

reason: Verified the upstream transport timeout tests remain aligned with the new timeout constants.
notes: No code changes required after the timeout update.

reason: Raised upstream proxy timeout values to give backends more time before RouteGate returns a maintenance-page 503.
notes: Connect/TLS timeout set to 10s, response header timeout set to 30s, expect-continue timeout set to 5s.

reason: Confirmed that the router’s 503 behavior comes from upstream failure handling in the reverse proxy path, so timeout tuning is the correct low-risk adjustment.
notes: No code changes in this file for this step.

reason: Confirmed that upstream failures are surfaced as the router-generated 503 maintenance page rather than a backend-provided response.

reason: Adjusted upstream timeout constants to give proxied backends more time before the router emits its own 503 maintenance response.
notes: Connect/TLS timeout set to 5s, response header timeout set to 15s, expect-continue timeout set to 2s.

reason: Confirmed the router forwards requests with ReverseProxy and only emits 503 via maintenance handling when upstream access fails or times out.
notes: No behavior changes made here.
